stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 drug treatment options. The probability of favorable patient genotype is uncertain, estimated between 44% and 63%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Drug A: $265K in good patient genotype, $56K in bad patient genotype. Drug B: $79K in good patient genotype, $21K in bad patient genotype. Drug C: $86K in good patient genotype, $23K in bad patient genotype. combination therapy: $148K in good patient genotype, $52K in bad patient genotype. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.54: best option is Drug A with EV=$168.9K. Drug A is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [148.0, 187.7].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
Drug A is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
